div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Persia (Philip)A similar telegram, No. 35, Feb. 14, 1928, 5 p.m., was sent to the Ambassador in Great Britain (file No. 791.003/67c).
div.p:
11. Department’s 8, January 31, 3 p.m.Not printed. Today the following points were brought orally to the attention of the British Ambassador: (1)
The Department is in substantial agreement with the position of the British Government explained in the 10 points telegraphed in the Department’s 6, January 26, 6 p.m. (2)
The Department would not be adverse to cooperating with the British Government so far as possible on the basis of its 10 points. The Department would particularly welcome a closer cooperation at Teheran between the two Legations. (3)
The Department would be glad to be more precisely informed as to the margin allowed to Clive’s discretion for negotiation and, also,
as to how Great Britain proposes meeting the situation which will arise after May 10, 1928, if a new treaty is not then in effect. (4)
The Department is considering having conversations soon of a similar character with the French, German, and Italian Ambassadors.
